ncaaD1.notes: 3rd all-time. Four consecutive 1991–94 (NCAA record). Runner-up 1997, 2019. Claudio Reyna, John Harkes.
Top 5 all-time. Big Ten Tourn champs 2025. #1 kinesiology nationally.
Runner-up 2016. 4 MLS picks in 2025 draft alone. ACC powerhouse.
Only Big West D1 title ever. Manu Duah #1 overall 2025 MLS pick.
Beat FIU in the 1996 final. Only Big East program to win a D1 title. Dr Dave Masur 2× National CoY.
Lost 1996 final to St John's. AAC Championship Final 2022, 2024, 2025. 14 MLS picks all-time.
First ever ACC Tournament title 2025. No NCAA D1 championship. FC Dallas pipeline. 5+ MLS picks last 5yr.
Kiefer returning. 20 MLS draftees in first USF tenure. Tampa warm climate.
Battery (USL) pro connection. Program improving year on year under Wiggans.
Best D1 MF opening in guide — 6 of 9 MFs clear before 2027. USL pathway building.

ncaaD1.dividerLabel: Ivy League (no athletic scholarships — need-based aid only)

ncaaD1.notes: 1999, 2001, 2010, 2014, 2018, 2021, 2024, 2025. First ever Ivy League + Tournament double 2025.
2023 Ivy Tournament champion. No athletic scholarships.

ncaaD2.notes: 2024 national champions (20-0-2 undefeated season). Coach Rootes 2024 Natl Staff of Year. Runner-up 2017.
Won D2 titles before promotion to D1 in 1987. Runner-up 1980, 1985. Now D1 AAC.
Beat West Chester 2-1. Runner-up 2000. Pre-PT Seminar (HPS450) in degree curriculum.

ncaaD2.dividerLabel: No D2 title — conference & notable results

ncaaD2.notes: Ranked #2 nationally 2025. Pre-PT concentration in degree title. Best D2 opening pathway for Olivier.
DPT program on campus — possible direct undergraduate-to-DPT articulation.
Austin FC pipeline. Possession-based coach Brian Young. Strong Texas market.
Most affordable LA option ~$28k. Strong academic environment for PT pathway.
Jack Allan (AUS) on current roster. CAMC hospital clinical partnership for pre-PT.
Full scholarship potential. Brian Harvey (founder, NAIA HOF) played pro in Australia.
Full scholarship potential. Health sciences campus. Fort Lauderdale warm climate.
KIN405 Pre-PT required course — unique academic value. Academic-first choice.
#1 JUCO D1 transfer feeder nationally, all-time record. Heaviest international freshman recruiting of any JUCO in the guide (Germany, South Africa, Jamaica, Denmark, Switzerland, Korea, Australia).
Defeated Daytona State 1-0 in the 2025 final. 2025 First-Team All-American Fabian Arroyo. Two Australians on current roster (Brisbane, Melbourne).
2024 runner-up. "NJCAA's most dominant JUCO program nationally." Confirmed pro pipeline — USL Championship, USL League One, MLS Next Pro alumni.
Lost 1-0 to Indian Hills in the 2025 final. 3 2025 NJCAA All-Americans.
3 2025 NJCAA All-Americans (1st/2nd/3rd team). KJCCC regular-season champions.
2025 NJCAA DI National Tournament qualifier, KJCCC runner-up. 1 2025 NJCAA All-American.
Ranked #2 nationally NJCAA DI, Oct 2025. Back-to-back Region I champions.
NJCAA DI National Tournament semifinalist, back-to-back tournament appearances (2024-25, 2025-26). 13 players advanced to 4-year programs in 2025.
2025 First-Team NJCAA All-Americans Efraims Valutadatils (Latvia) and Caden Johnson. 100% roster turnover before 2027 — clean slate entry.
2024 NJCAA Division II National Champions — first title in program history (found and corrected v38.9). Near-100% international roster; 2-year transfer pathway to D1/D2/NAIA.
Proven pipeline → UCLA / UCSB. ~$9k/yr cost. 2yr stepping stone. No on-campus housing — commuter college.
Transfer → Barry / FIU. ~$11k/yr. South Florida warm climate. No on-campus housing — commuter college.

mlsDraft.notable: Kwaku Agyabeng (2026 R1 #4 SKC), Lukas Magnason (2026 R1 #13 RSL), Robbie Robinson (#1 overall 2020 + Hermann)
mlsDraft.allTime: Nation-leading 4 first-round picks in 2026 draft alone. 2× recent national champions.

mlsDraft.notable: Nick Dang (2025 R2), AJ Smith (2025 R3), Aidan Morris (2021 Columbus Crew, USMNT)
mlsDraft.allTime: Top 5 all-time MLS producers. 7 NCAA titles.

mlsDraft.allTime: Top 10 all-time, most decorated program history. 8 NCAA titles.

mlsDraft.notable: Jaylinn Mitchell (2026 R1), Cesar Ruvalcaba (2025 R2), Garrett McLaughlin (#8 overall 2020), Eddie Munjoma (Hermann Trophy)
mlsDraft.allTime: ACC elite pipeline, FC Dallas connections. First ever ACC Tourn title 2025.

mlsDraft.notable: Nicholas Cavallo (2025 R3), Grayson Doody (2024 R1 CF Montréal), Ahmed Longmire (#10 overall 2022)
mlsDraft.allTime: Top 8 all-time, LA MLS academy connections. 4 NCAA titles.

mlsDraft.notable: Travis Smith Jr. (2025 R1 Chicago), Liam O'Gara (2025 R3), Trace Alphin (2025 R2)
mlsDraft.allTime: 4 picks in single 2025 draft — highest single-draft rate in ACC that year.

mlsDraft.notable: Zack Steffen (USMNT WC goalkeeper), Graham Zusi (2014 WC goal), Omar Gonzalez, Taylor Twellman
mlsDraft.allTime: 49 MLS picks + 7 homegrown under Cirovski — most all-time of any college program.

mlsDraft.notable: 42 MLS signings under Wiese. Multiple DC United + NYCFC connections.
mlsDraft.allTime: 2019 national champions. D.C. MLS market proximity.

mlsDraft.notable: Michael Appiah (2025 R3 Inter Miami), Stephen Afrifa (#8 overall 2022 SKC)
mlsDraft.allTime: 14 MLS draft picks all-time. AAC Championship Final 3 of last 4 years.

mlsDraft.notable: Manu Duah (#1 overall 2025 — highest ever Big West pick)
mlsDraft.allTime: Strong Big West pipeline, Pacific MLS market. 1 NCAA title (2006).

mlsDraft.notable: Jahiem Wickham (2026 R3), Pedro Faife (2024 USL)
mlsDraft.allTime: Kiefer returning — 20 MLS draftees in first USF tenure. Rebuilding to AAC prominence.

mlsDraft.notable: Jon Gallagher (Austin FC), Paul Rothrock (Seattle Sounders), Philip Quinton (RSL)
mlsDraft.allTime: 27 MLS picks under Bobby Clark era. Chad Riley maintaining elite pipeline.

mlsDraft.notable: Multiple ACC All-Conference players to MLS/USL annually. Somoano building pipeline.
mlsDraft.allTime: Dorrance dynasty legacy. ACC Regular Season Champions 2023.

mlsDraft.notable: Kevin O'Toole (2022 R2 NYCFC), 5 total MLS players under Barlow
mlsDraft.allTime: Ivy pipeline growing — 2024–25 form surge. First Ivy League + Tournament double 2025.

mlsDraft.notable: Multiple Red Bulls / NYCFC academy connections. 1996 D1 national champions.
mlsDraft.allTime: NYC MLS network strongest of any school in guide. NYCFC + Red Bulls proximity.

mlsDraft.notable: Alumni signed with USL Championship, USL League One, and MLS Next Pro clubs — NYC metro pipeline (NYCFC, NY Red Bulls proximity)
mlsDraft.allTime: 3x NJCAA DI National Champions (2021-2023), 2024 runner-up — strongest confirmed pro pipeline of any JUCO in the guide, matching Princeton's recent picks count

mlsDraft.notable: 2025 AAC Tournament Champions. South Florida pro market (Inter Miami, Fort Lauderdale CF).
mlsDraft.allTime: Developing D1 AAC program. Best DPT pathway degree in guide. Warm climate match.

mlsDraft.notable: Multiple USL L1/L2 + European pro signings annually
mlsDraft.allTime: Best non-MLS pro pipelines in guide. Florida market.

mlsDraft.notable: Developing — USL League One/Two connections building
mlsDraft.allTime: Best D1 MF opening in guide — 6 of 9 MFs clear before 2027

mlsDraft.notable: Jack Allan (AUS) on current roster. USL pathway developing.
mlsDraft.allTime: Australian on squad — international pipeline confirmed

mlsDraft.school: Phoenix College / Tyler JC / Iowa Western / Indian Hills / Daytona State / Cowley CC / Barton CC / Arizona Western / EFSC / Pima CC / Mohave CC / Glendale CC / Dodge City CC / Neosho County CC / Johnson County CC / Southeastern CC / Iowa Lakes CC / Blinn College / Coastal Bend College / Northeast CC
mlsDraft.notable: No confirmed MLS SuperDraft picks in the last 5 years — value is national-tier competitiveness and D1/D2 transfer volume, not direct-to-MLS
mlsDraft.allTime: Phoenix College are the 2025 NJCAA DII National Champions (#1 nationally for 7 straight weeks) — the most decorated current result of any JUCO in this group. Combined with Tyler JC (6x champion 2009-2017), Indian Hills (2025 DI champion), Daytona State (2025 DI runner-up), Arizona Western (#2 nationally Oct 2025), and Johnson County CC's 2025 Region 6/9 tournament title. All 19 schools are currently nationally ranked or regionally dominant (NJCAA DI/DII). 100% roster turnover before 2027 — clean slate entry at every one.

mlsDraft.school: St Edward's / Cal State LA / OCU / Keiser / Chapman
mlsDraft.notable: USL / semi-pro pathways. Development-focused programs.
mlsDraft.allTime: Pro pipeline secondary to academic/PT pathway value for Olivier

mlsDraft.school: Santa Monica / Miami Dade / Angelina College / LSU Eunice / Nassau CC / Ulster County CC / Suffolk CC / Westchester CC
mlsDraft.notable: Transfer stepping stones — pro pathway via destination school or solid regional (non-nationally-ranked) competitiveness, not a national-title program
mlsDraft.allTime: Pipeline value comes from UCLA/UCSB/Barry/FIU transfer destinations or solid regional (non-nationally-ranked) records rather than a national title. Suffolk County CC won the 2025 NJCAA Region 15 Championship and is the only Lower New York JUCO in the guide with a confirmed dedicated Exercise Science degree. Nassau CC had the best 2025 regular-season record in Region 15 (10-2); Ulster County CC reached the 2025 Region 15 Championship final.
